title: [ValueTracking] Conservative nosync check prevents vectorization
author: kshitijvp
description:
Conservative nosync check in ValueTracking.cpp returns false
causing potentially faulting load preventing vectorization.
Instead check if any instructions between Assume Instruction
and Ctx Instruction are synchronizing.

Fixes #180180

tests.test_body:
declare void @llvm.assume(i1)

define void @deref_assumption_in_header_constant_trip_count(ptr noalias noundef %a, ptr noalias %b, ptr noalias %c) nofree nosync{
;
entry:
  br label %loop.header

loop.header:
  %iv = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %loop.latch ]
  %gep.a = getelementptr i32, ptr %a, i64 %iv
  call void @llvm.assume(i1 true) [ "align"(ptr %gep.a, i64 4), "dereferenceable"(ptr %gep.a, i64 4) ]
  %gep.b = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %b, i64 %iv
  %l.b = load i32, ptr %gep.b, align 4
  %c.1 = icmp sge i32 %l.b, 0
  br i1 %c.1, label %loop.latch, label %loop.then

loop.then:
  %l.a = load i32, ptr %gep.a, align 4
  br label %loop.latch

loop.latch:
  %merge = phi i32 [ %l.a, %loop.then ], [ %l.b, %loop.header ]
  %gep.c = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %c, i64 %iv
  store i32 %merge, ptr %gep.c, align 4
  %iv.next = add nuw nsw i64 %iv, 1
  %ec = icmp eq i64 %iv.next, 1000
  br i1 %ec, label %exit, label %loop.header

exit:
  ret void
}

define void @align_deref_assumption_in_header_constant_trip_count_loop_invariant_ptr(ptr noalias noundef %a, ptr noalias %b, ptr noalias %c) nofree nosync{
;
entry:

loop.header:

  %gep.b = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %b, i64 %iv
  %l.b = load i32, ptr %gep.b, align 4
  %c.1 = icmp sge i32 %l.b, 0
  br i1 %c.1, label %loop.latch, label %loop.then

loop.then:
  %l.a = load i32, ptr %a, align 4
  br label %loop.latch

loop.latch:
  %merge = phi i32 [ %l.a, %loop.then ], [ %l.b, %loop.header ]
  %gep.c = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %c, i64 %iv
  store i32 %merge, ptr %gep.c, align 4
  %iv.next = add nuw nsw i64 %iv, 1
  %ec = icmp eq i64 %iv.next, 1000
  br i1 %ec, label %exit, label %loop.header

exit:
  ret void
}

; %a may be freed between the dereferenceable assumption and accesses.
define void @may_free_align_deref_assumption_in_header_constant_trip_count_loop_invariant_ptr(ptr noalias noundef %a, ptr noalias %b, ptr noalias %c) {
;
entry:
  call void @llvm.assume(i1 true) [ "align"(ptr %a, i64 4), "dereferenceable"(ptr %a, i64 4) ]
  call void @may_free()
  br label %loop.header

loop.header:
  %iv = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %loop.latch ]
  %gep.b = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %b, i64 %iv
  %l.b = load i32, ptr %gep.b, align 4
  %c.1 = icmp sge i32 %l.b, 0
  br i1 %c.1, label %loop.latch, label %loop.then

loop.then:
  %l.a = load i32, ptr %a, align 4
  br label %loop.latch

loop.latch:
  %merge = phi i32 [ %l.a, %loop.then ], [ %l.b, %loop.header ]
  %gep.c = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %c, i64 %iv
  store i32 %merge, ptr %gep.c, align 4
  %iv.next = add nuw nsw i64 %iv, 1
  %ec = icmp eq i64 %iv.next, 1000
  br i1 %ec, label %exit, label %loop.header

exit:
  ret void
}

; %a may be freed between the dereferenceable assumption and accesses.
define void @may_free_local_ptr_align_deref_assumption_in_header_constant_trip_count_loop_invariant_ptr(ptr noalias %b, ptr noalias %c) nofree nosync {
;
entry:
  %a = call ptr @get_ptr()
  call void @llvm.assume(i1 true) [ "align"(ptr %a, i64 4), "dereferenceable"(ptr %a, i64 4) ]
  call void @may_free()
  br label %loop.header

loop.header:
  %iv = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %loop.latch ]
  %gep.b = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %b, i64 %iv
  %l.b = load i32, ptr %gep.b, align 4
  %c.1 = icmp sge i32 %l.b, 0
  br i1 %c.1, label %loop.latch, label %loop.then

loop.then:
  %l.a = load i32, ptr %a, align 4
  br label %loop.latch

loop.latch:
  %merge = phi i32 [ %l.a, %loop.then ], [ %l.b, %loop.header ]
  %gep.c = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %c, i64 %iv
  store i32 %merge, ptr %gep.c, align 4
  %iv.next = add nuw nsw i64 %iv, 1
  %ec = icmp eq i64 %iv.next, 1000
  br i1 %ec, label %exit, label %loop.header

exit:
  ret void
}

declare ptr @get_ptr()
declare void @may_free()

define void @deref_assumption_in_header_constant_trip_count_nofree_via_context(ptr noalias noundef %a, ptr noalias %b, ptr noalias %c) nosync {
;

  br label %loop.header

loop.header:
  %iv = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %loop.latch ]
  %gep.a = getelementptr i32, ptr %a, i64 %iv
  %gep.b = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %b, i64 %iv
  %l.b = load i32, ptr %gep.b, align 4
  %c.1 = icmp sge i32 %l.b, 0
  br i1 %c.1, label %loop.latch, label %loop.then

loop.then:
  %l.a = load i32, ptr %gep.a, align 4
  br label %loop.latch

loop.latch:
  %merge = phi i32 [ %l.a, %loop.then ], [ %l.b, %loop.header ]
  %gep.c = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %c, i64 %iv
  store i32 %merge, ptr %gep.c, align 4
  %iv.next = add nuw nsw i64 %iv, 1
  %ec = icmp eq i64 %iv.next, 1000
  br i1 %ec, label %exit, label %loop.header

exit:
  ret void
}

define void @deref_assumption_in_header_constant_trip_count_may_free(ptr noalias noundef %a, ptr noalias %b, ptr noalias %c) nosync {
;
entry:
  call void @llvm.assume(i1 true) [ "align"(ptr %a, i64 4), "dereferenceable"(ptr %a, i64 4000) ]
  call void @may_free()
  br label %loop.header

loop.header:
  %iv = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %loop.latch ]
  %gep.a = getelementptr i32, ptr %a, i64 %iv
  %gep.b = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %b, i64 %iv
  %l.b = load i32, ptr %gep.b, align 4
  %c.1 = icmp sge i32 %l.b, 0
  br i1 %c.1, label %loop.latch, label %loop.then

loop.then:
  %l.a = load i32, ptr %gep.a, align 4
  br label %loop.latch

loop.latch:
  %merge = phi i32 [ %l.a, %loop.then ], [ %l.b, %loop.header ]
  %gep.c = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %c, i64 %iv
  store i32 %merge, ptr %gep.c, align 4
  %iv.next = add nuw nsw i64 %iv, 1
  %ec = icmp eq i64 %iv.next, 1000
  br i1 %ec, label %exit, label %loop.header

exit:
  ret void
}

```
define void @loop_contains_store_assumed_bounds(ptr noalias %array, ptr readonly %pred, i32 %n) {
; CHECK-LABEL: LV: Checking a loop in 'loop_contains_store_assumed_bounds'
; CHECK:       LV: Not vectorizing: Loop may fault.
entry:
  %n_bytes = mul nuw nsw i32 %n, 2
  call void @llvm.assume(i1 true) [ "align"(ptr %pred, i64 2), "dereferenceable"(ptr %pred, i32 %n_bytes) ]
  %tc = sext i32 %n to i64
  br label %for.body

for.body:
  %iv = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %for.inc ]
  %st.addr = getelementptr inbounds nuw i16, ptr %array, i64 %iv
  %data = load i16, ptr %st.addr, align 2
  %inc = add nsw i16 %data, 1
  store i16 %inc, ptr %st.addr, align 2
  %ee.addr = getelementptr inbounds nuw i16, ptr %pred, i64 %iv
  %ee.val = load i16, ptr %ee.addr, align 2
  %ee.cond = icmp sgt i16 %ee.val, 500
  br i1 %ee.cond, label %exit, label %for.inc

for.inc:
  %iv.next = add nuw nsw i64 %iv, 1
  %counted.cond = icmp eq i64 %iv.next, %tc
  br i1 %counted.cond, label %exit, label %for.body

exit:
  ret void
}
declare void @init_mem(ptr, i64);
declare i64 @get_an_unknown_offset();
```

```
#14 0x000058ee9814008b llvm::isDereferenceableAndAlignedInLoop(llvm::LoadInst*, llvm::Loop*, llvm::ScalarEvolution&, llvm::DominatorTree&, llvm::AssumptionCache*, llvm::SmallVectorImpl<llvm::SCEVPredicate const*>*) (/usr/local/bin/opt+0x542608b)

#29 0x00007c49d4c2a1ca __libc_start_call_main ./csu/../sysdeps/nptl/libc_start_call_main.h:74:3
#30 0x00007c49d4c2a28b call_init ./csu/../csu/libc-start.c:128:20
#31 0x00007c49d4c2a28b __libc_start_main ./csu/../csu/libc-start.c:347:5
#32 0x000058ee97c309a5 _start (/usr/local/bin/opt+0x4f169a5)
```
This is triggering in evaluatePtrAddRecAtMaxBTCWillNotWrap due to type mismatch.
This function calls getUMaxExpr here
https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/blob/fa5f4afba9d8b5eeba30ddce25043f4e818686aa/llvm/lib/Analysis/LoopAccessAnalysis.cpp#L254

with the operands DerefBytesSCEV and SE.getSCEV(DerefRK.IRArgValue) which are of different types (i64 and i32 respectively).

This seems unrelated to my change, it must be exposing this.

comments.author: kshitijvp
comments.body:
Adding this instead of directly calling `getUMaxExpr()`
in llvm/lib/Analysis/LoopAccessAnalysis.cpp fixes the assertion failure.

```
const SCEV *DerefRKSCEV = SE.getSCEV(DerefRK.IRArgValue);  // i32 SCEV
Type *CommonTy = SE.getWiderType(DerefBytesSCEV->getType(), DerefRKSCEV->getType());
DerefBytesSCEV = SE.getNoopOrAnyExtend(DerefBytesSCEV, CommonTy);  // extend to i64
DerefRKSCEV = SE.getNoopOrAnyExtend(DerefRKSCEV, CommonTy);        // extend to i64
DerefBytesSCEV = SE.getUMaxExpr(DerefBytesSCEV, DerefRKSCEV);      // now both i64
```
